changes.description: API: New insert_blocks action — insert blocks at a specific position in a page using after_block_id or position. Existing blocks shift down automatically.
API: New update_block action — patch a single block's content, type, checked state, or indent level in-place without affecting its position.
API: New move_collection action — move a collection to a different workspace via the API.
API: workspace_name is now supported on move_collection, rename_workspace, and delete_workspace — use a partial name instead of an ID for all workspace-targeting actions.
API docs and downloadable SKILL.md updated to reflect all new actions with full examples.
API: New workspace actions — create, rename, and delete workspaces programmatically via Master Key with workspace management permissions.
API docs updated to document all workspace actions with full curl examples.
Downloadable SKILL.md now includes workspace operations for AI agent integrations.
Chrome extension "Open Parchment" button now correctly deep-links to the newly saved page instead of the home screen.
Workspace switcher dropdown buttons are now left-aligned.
Multiple workspaces — create, rename, and delete named workspaces to organize your collections.
Workspace switcher in your profile menu and top bar lets you jump between workspaces instantly.
Move any collection to a different workspace from the context menu in both list and board view.
Trash, page counts, and Empty Trash are now scoped to the active workspace.
Always-visible top bar — the header stays persistent regardless of whether a page is open.
New accounts get Personal and Work workspaces out of the box. Existing users have all collections migrated to a Personal workspace.
Board view now has a persistent top bar with your profile menu, matching the style of List view.
Page timestamps now show date and time (e.g. Apr 6, 11:07 PM) with a "Last Updated" tooltip on hover.
Page type badge is now hidden for blank pages — only shows for notes, checklist, and roadmap types.
Pages opened in Board view now have a Share button in the modal header — toggle sharing, set public or private access, and copy the link without leaving the board.
Collections can now be dragged to reorder in both List view (sidebar) and Board view.
New API action: reorder_collections — set the display order of your collections programmatically.
New API action: move_page — move a page to a different collection via API.
API docs and downloadable SKILL.md now include move_page, rename_collection, and reorder_collections with full examples.
Board view drag/drop column reordering restored after being lost in a previous refactor.
Fixed trash modal in board view not being scrollable when many items were present.
Fixed trash page in list view stretching full width — now constrained to the standard page container.
Added List/Board view toggle to the trash page in list view.
Added Socket.dev supply chain security monitoring — every dependency change is scanned for malicious packages, typosquatting, and compromised maintainers.
Upgraded react-router-dom to 6.30.3, resolving 3 high severity vulnerabilities (XSS and Open Redirect).
Added Snyk security scanning to our CI pipeline for continuous dependency and code vulnerability monitoring.
New Board view — a Trello-style horizontal layout showing all your collections as columns. Toggle between List and Board views with the pill at the bottom of the screen.
Add new collections from anywhere with the floating + button in the bottom-right corner.
Manage your trash directly from the Board view with the Trash button in the bottom-left.
Delete collections directly from their card header in Board view.
Adding a new collection auto-scrolls the board so the new column is always in view.
Footer controls (Feedback, toggle, Trash) adapt to icon-only or shorter labels on mobile to prevent overlap.
Parchment Chrome Extension is here! Save recipes, YouTube videos (with transcripts), and articles to Parchment in one click from any page.
YouTube videos: fetch the full transcript via TranscriptAPI and optionally generate an AI summary before saving to your YouTube Videos collection.
AI-powered page detection — the extension automatically recognizes recipes, YouTube videos, and general articles, routing each to the right collection.
AI summaries supported via OpenAI, Anthropic Claude, or Google Gemini (free tier available). Add your API key in extension Settings.
After saving, "Open Parchment" deep-links directly to the newly created page — no hunting through the sidebar.
Extension is available now on GitHub while awaiting official Chrome Web Store approval. Install instructions at theparchment.app.
Move pages between collections — drag and drop on desktop, or use the ⋮ menu on any page and choose "Move to…" on mobile.
URLs in API-inserted blocks are now automatically clickable on page load — no need to click in and out of the block first.
Hold Ctrl and hover a link to see the pointer cursor, indicating it's clickable. Ctrl+click opens the link.
New API action: move_page — moves a page to a different collection by page_id and target collection_id.
Multi-select blocks — hover any block to reveal a checkbox, click to enter selection mode. Select multiple blocks and delete them all at once.
Ctrl+A (or Cmd+A) selects all blocks on the page at once. Press Ctrl+A again to deselect all, or hit Escape to exit selection mode.
Shift+click to select a range of blocks between two checkboxes. Select All / Deselect All buttons in the action bar for quick control.

title: Nested Lists, Keyboard Navigation & Long Title Support

changes.description: Nested list indentation — press TAB on any numbered or bullet list item to indent it into a sub-list. SHIFT+TAB outdents. Numbered sub-lists automatically use letter labels (a., b., c.) at level 2 and roman numerals (i., ii., iii.) at level 3+.
Slash command keyboard navigation — use the arrow keys to move through the slash command menu and press Enter to select. No more reaching for the mouse.
Page titles no longer truncate when they're too long — the title field now grows to wrap across multiple lines, just like a regular heading.

title: Resizable Sidebar, Group Blocks & Bug Fixes

changes.description: Group blocks — use /group to create a container block that groups related content together. Delete the whole group at once, or style it with a custom background and border color.
Group block styling — click the pencil icon on any group to pick a background and border color from a curated palette.
Resizable sidebar — drag the border between the sidebar and main content to make the sidebar wider or narrower. Your preferred width is saved automatically.
Pages are now visually indented under their collection with a subtle tree-style border, making the hierarchy between collections and pages much clearer.
Fixed a bug where closing the sidebar on mobile would briefly show a fullscreen black panel before the layout settled.

title: Page Sharing, Beta Tester Program & PWA

changes.description: Page Sharing — share any page via a public link anyone can view, or restrict it to specific Parchment users by email. Shared pages are fully read-only for viewers.
Shared with me — pages privately shared with your email address now appear in a dedicated sidebar section.
Beta Tester program — opt in from Settings to get early access to features still being tested. Check the boxes for the features you want to try.
Progressive Web App (PWA) — Parchment can now be installed to your home screen on mobile and desktop for a native app-like experience.
Collections are now collapsed by default when you open the app.
New users are now redirected to Settings after signup with the API key form pre-opened and agent-friendly permissions pre-selected — making it easy to connect an AI agent right away.
Each API key card now shows a "Download SKILL.md" button — downloads a ready-to-use skill file for your AI agent with full API reference, curl examples, and block type docs.
Fixed a bug where new accounts would sometimes get multiple duplicate "Getting Started" collections created on first login.
Trash items are now automatically and permanently deleted 30 days after being moved to trash.
Trash page now shows a warning banner and a "X days left" countdown on each item instead of the raw deletion date.
New favicon — the Parchment logo is now shown in browser tabs.
API docs now fully document all block types, HTML content format, inline formatting (bold, italic, strikethrough, color, links), and data export.
API docs crawler-friendly noscript block updated to match the full public docs — AI bots can now read complete API documentation.
Security: block HTML content is now sanitized with DOMPurify before rendering, preventing potential XSS injection via the API.
Fixed Admin Reports page failing to load with Access denied error.
Fixed Settings → Export Data button pointing to the wrong server.
Fixed API Docs page showing an incorrect base URL.
Double-click (or long-press on mobile) any page or collection name in the sidebar to rename it inline.
Fixed an issue on mobile where tapping outside the rename input wouldn't exit edit mode.
Inline formatting toolbar — select text to bold, italicize, strikethrough, change color, or turn it into a link.
Auto-linkify — URLs typed into blocks automatically become clickable links when you leave the block.
Ctrl+click (Cmd+click on Mac) to open links — regular clicks place the cursor for editing.
Mobile-friendly toolbar positioning — on touch devices the toolbar appears below the selection to avoid clashing with native OS menus.
Ctrl+Z / Cmd+Z now undoes the last block deletion — no need to click the toast, just press undo like you normally would.
Added Trash for pages and collections — deleted items are now soft-deleted and can be restored or permanently removed from the Trash view.
Block-level undo — deleting a block now shows a toast with an Undo action to instantly recover it.
Fixed an issue where typing in blocks was being overwritten in real-time by the auto-save sync, causing characters to disappear while editing.
Fixed navigation bug where clicking a page in the sidebar while viewing Trash would not navigate back to the editor.
Pages & Collections — organise your thoughts into pages grouped by collections.
Block-based editor with support for headings, lists, to-dos, quotes, code blocks, and dividers.
Slash commands — type "/" to quickly change block types.
REST API with granular API key permissions and rate limiting.
Real-time sync across devices with optimistic local updates.
